
Reviewed Grok social signals and arXiv papers. arXiv papers are all generic AI/ML research with no Claude-specific practical tips. Grok signals include several Claude-specific workflow items, but most either duplicate existing README entries (Projects, XML prompting) or link to unverifiable/fabricated URLs. One signal stands out as novel and verifiable: the advanced prompting patterns guide covering chain-of-thought and scratchpad techniques for Claude 3.5 Sonnet, which is distinct from the XML-only tip already listed.

## Suggestions

- [Advanced Claude Techniques: Scratchpads & Chain-of-Thought](https://www.promptingguide.ai/models/claude-3-5-sonnet) — Documented best practices for eliciting extended reasoning from Claude via scratchpad-style prompts and chain-of-thought patterns, with concrete techniques for reducing errors on multi-step tasks.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Applicable to a broad audience (not highly domain-specific or niche), Novel or non-obvious — not a basic feature already in official docs
